intro.uz: Sarguzasht — hayotni yangilik, sayohat va tavakkal bilan toʻldirish. Tim Ferriss buni “Adventure Deficit Disorder” (sarguzasht yetishmovchiligi) deb ataydi: koʻp kattalar bir xil zerikarli tartibda qotib qoladi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatik mavzusi: artikllar 3 — a/an, the va artiklsiz shaklning toʻliq mustahkamlanishi, hamda hikoya zamonlari — past continuous va past perfect. Michelle haqidagi POV hikoyada voqeani qatlamma-qatlam eshitasiz.
intro.en: Adventure — filling life with novelty, travel and risk. Tim Ferriss calls its absence “Adventure Deficit Disorder”: many adults get stuck in the same boring routine. This week's two grammar topics: articles 3 — the full consolidation of a/an, the and the zero article, plus narrative tenses — past continuous and past perfect. You'll hear the story unfold in layers in the POV about Michelle.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida sayohat va sarguzasht haqida gapirganda a/an, the va artiklsiz shaklni toʻgʻri tanlab qoʻya olasiz, hamda oʻz sarguzashtingizni past continuous (I was travelling…) va past perfect (I had never seen…) yordamida qatlam-qatlam soʻzlab bera olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can choose a/an, the and the zero article correctly when talking about travel and adventure, and tell your own adventure in layers using the past continuous (I was travelling…) and the past perfect (I had never seen…).

grammar.titleUz: Artikllar 3: a/an, the, artiklsiz — toʻliq mustahkamlash
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Speaking & Writing — accurate a/an, the and zero article
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1→B2: I can choose a/an, the or no article correctly in connected speech
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida artikl umuman yoʻq: “sayohatga chiqdim” — bu “a trip”mi yoki “the trip”mi, faqat kontekstdan bilinadi. Ingliz tili buni majburan koʻrsatadi: birinchi marta — a trip (qaysidir biri), keyin oʻsha narsa — the trip (aynan oʻsha). Umumiy maʼnodagi sanaladigan koʻplik va sanalmaydigan otlarda esa artiklsiz (ø): I love ø travelling.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I went to airport and took plane. ✅ I went to the airport and took the plane. Vaziyatdan aniq (yagona, biladigan) narsa → the; sanaladigan birlik ot hech qachon artiklsiz qolmaydi — hech boʻlmasa a/an kerak.

examples.en: I took a trip to the mountains.
We waited at the airport for hours.

exercises.prompt: Last summer I took ___ amazing trip to Thailand.
exercises.hintUz: birinchi marta tilga olinyapti + keyingi tovush unli (amazing) → an

exercises.prompt: We got to ___ airport two hours early.

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): eng yaxshi sayohatingiz haqida uchta jumla — “I took a … . The … was … . I love ø … .” — uchala tanlovni (a / the / artiklsiz) ishlating.
exercises.hintUz: birinchi marta → a/an; aynan oʻsha → the; umumiy → artiklsiz

grammar.titleUz: Hikoya zamonlari: past continuous va past perfect
grammar.titleEn: Narrative tenses — past continuous & past perfect
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 2 — tell a story in vivid layers
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1→B2: I can narrate a past event with background and earlier actions
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekcha hikoyada koʻpincha hammasi bitta oʻtgan zamonda ketadi: “ishlardim, boʻron boshlandi, avval koʻrmagandim”. Ingliz tili vaqt qatlamlarini alohida shakllar bilan ajratadi: davom etayotgani → was working (oʻzbekcha “-yotgan edim”), undan oldingisi → had worked (oʻzbekcha “-gan edim”).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ When the storm started, I travelled in the mountains. ✅ When the storm started, I was travelling in the mountains. Fon — davom etayotgan ish → past continuous (was/were + V-ing); undan oldin boʻlgan ish → past perfect (had + V3): ✅ Before I moved, I had never seen the sea.

examples.en: I was travelling when the storm started.
examples.uz: Boʻron boshlanganda men sayohat qilayotgan edim.

examples.en: By the time she quit, the stress had built for years.
examples.uz: U ishdan ketganda, zoʻriqish yillar davomida toʻplanib boʻlgandi.

examples.en: She had never seen the ocean before she moved to Thailand.
examples.uz: Tailandga koʻchishidan oldin u okeanni hech koʻrmagandi.

exercises.prompt: I ___ through the mountains when the storm started.
exercises.hintUz: davom etayotgan fon + when → past continuous (was + V-ing)

exercises.prompt: By the time she quit, the stress ___ for years.
exercises.hintUz: oʻtmishdagi paytdan yana oldin boʻlgan ish → past perfect (had + V3)

exercises.prompt: She had never ___ the ocean before that trip.

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): kichik sarguzashtingizni bitta “fon + hodisa” bilan boshlang — “I was … when …” — keyin bitta “orqaga qadam” qoʻshing: “… because I had …”.
exercises.hintUz: fon → was/were + V-ing; oldingi ish → had + V3

vocab.uz: ichki suhbat (miyadagi ovoz, oʻz-oʻzi bilan gaplashish)
vocab.defEn: the conversation happening inside your head
vocab.example: Other people's opinions become part of your internal dialogue.

vocab.uz: sarguzasht yetishmovchiligi (kattalarda: hayotda yetarli hayajon yoʻqligi)
vocab.defEn: a jokey name for adults who don't have enough adventure in their life
vocab.example: Tim Ferriss says most adults suffer from adventure deficit disorder.

vocab.defEn: money that comes in automatically without you working for it
vocab.example: He dreamed of automated income from a website.

vocab.uz: ijobiy pul oqimi (kiruvchi pul chiquvchidan koʻp)
vocab.defEn: more money coming in than going out
vocab.example: The business finally had strong positive cash flow.

vocab.uz: asabiy portlash, hissiy inqiroz (oʻzini boshqara olmay qolish)
vocab.defEn: an emotional crisis in which you lose all control
vocab.example: After years of stress she finally had a meltdown.

vocab.uz: ogʻir ish yuki (bajarish kerak boʻlgan koʻp ish)
vocab.defEn: a large amount of work you have to do
vocab.example: Her workload was too heavy, so she was always tired.

vocab.defEn: not knowing what will happen, which causes fear
vocab.example: We keep busy to avoid fear-inducing uncertainty.

vocab.defEn: the history or timeline of events in order
vocab.example: If you look at my chronology, the change came late.

vocab.uz: muqobil mashgʻulotlar (ish oʻrnini bosadigan boshqa qiziqishlar)
vocab.defEn: other things to do that can replace work
vocab.example: He had no alternative activities, so he just kept working.

vocab.uz: odatiy tartibni buzmoq, bir xillikdan chiqmoq
vocab.defEn: to stop doing the same routine and do something new
vocab.example: Travel helps me break my pattern every year.

englishpod.title: The Weekend – Going to the Beach
englishpod.titleUz: Dam olish kuni — dengiz boʻyiga borish

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda bir oila shahardan chiqib, dengiz boʻyida kun oʻtkazadi — bolalar suvga tushadi, qumdan qalʼa quradi, meduza uchratishadi. AJ’ning “Adventure” gʻoyasiga mos: kichik sayohat ham odatiy tartibni buzadi va hayotga hayajon olib keladi. Sizningcha, oddiy plyaj kuni ham “sarguzasht” boʻla oladimi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue a family leaves the city for a day at the beach — the kids swim, build a sandcastle and meet a jellyfish. It fits AJ's “Adventure” theme: even a small trip breaks the routine and brings excitement. Do you think an ordinary beach day can count as an adventure?

keyVocab.uz: sochilib yotgan, u yer-bu yerda tarqalgan
joylashmoq, tayyorlab olmoq (masalan, dam olish joyini)

keyVocab.defEn: a small model of a castle made of sand
to go in a direction away from the group
a raised area of sand near the surface of the water

quiz.q: Archaeologists in Jordan discovered what they believe is the oldest remains of bread. How old is this bread?
quiz.qUz: Iordaniyadagi arxeologlar eng qadimgi non qoldiqlarini topishdi. Bu non necha yillik?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: b) 14,000 yillik. Rob c) 5,500 dedi va xato qildi. Neil aytadi: “The answer was 14,000 years” — bu dunyodagi eng qadimgi non qoldigʻi.

vocab.defEn: to suddenly become popular and successful
the parts of a country outside the major cities

vocab.uz: diqqatni jalb qilmoq, hayolni band etmoq
vocab.defEn: to make people interested for more than a short time
real and genuine, not a fake or a copy
food prepared or reheated in a microwave oven

funEnglish.title: Saying yes to adventure: seeking discomfort around the world

speakingPrompt.en: Tell the story of an adventure or a trip you took. Where did you go, and what were you doing when something unexpected happened? What had you never done before that day?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: artikllar (a trip, the airport, ø adventure) va hikoya zamonlari — past continuous (I was travelling…) va past perfect (I had never … before).
